task_completion.visual_evidence: The Odoo dashboard or Apps menu is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Open the failed quality check for 'Large Cabinet'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Quality Check form for the product 'Large Cabinet' is open, and its status is shown as 'Failed'.

task_completion.description: Initiate the creation of a Quality Alert from the failed check.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Quality Alert form is open. It should be linked to the 'Large Cabinet' product or the specific failed quality check.

task_completion.description: Set the Quality Alert title to 'Cabinet Structural Defect'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title field of the Quality Alert form contains the text 'Cabinet Structural Defect'.

task_completion.description: Add the tag 'Structural' to the Quality Alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag 'Structural' is visible in the Tags field of the Quality Alert form.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Odoo graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Odoo web interface being clicked and typed into, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected answers into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs 'Cabinet Structural Defect' and 'Structural' are entered into the actual Odoo web forms, not a separate text file.

integrity.description: The agent created the alert through the proper Odoo workflow.
integrity.visual_evidence: The alert is created from within the Quality module, originating from the failed check, rather than using developer tools or API calls.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target product is 'Large Cabinet'. The quality alert must originate from a failed quality check for this product. The expected alert title is 'Cabinet Structural Defect' and the expected tag is 'Structural'.
